# psy-core LLM map

psy-core gives AI agent memory writes tamper-evident receipts. It wraps supported memory providers and agent memory surfaces, writes paired intent/result audit rows, hashes each canonical row into a SQLite-backed chain, and seals the tail with HMAC.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/jethros-projects/psy-core
Core package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/psy-core
Hermes plugin: https://pypi.org/project/psy-core-hermes/
OpenClaw plugin: https://www.npmjs.com/package/psy-core-openclaw

## Read order

1. README.md for the product shape and install paths.
2. SECURITY.md before making claims about what verification proves.
3. The relevant adapter or plugin README before changing an integration.
4. AGENTS.md before modifying code or docs as an agent.

## Core docs

- README.md: product overview, install flow, adapter coverage, CLI reference, configuration, guarantees, repository map.
- SECURITY.md: coordinated disclosure, threat model, what psy-core detects, what it does not detect, operational notes.
- CONTRIBUTING.md: local contribution rules.
- AGENTS.md: agent-facing repo operating guide.
- CHANGELOG.md: release notes.
- RELEASING.md: release procedure.

## Source map

- src/auditor.ts: two-phase audit writer, intent/result flow, hash-chain append behavior.
- src/store.ts: SQLite persistence for audit rows and metadata.
- src/verify.ts: chain, archive, orphan, and seal verification.
- src/seal.ts: HMAC seal key and sealed-head pointer handling.
- src/canonical.ts: canonical JSON serialization.
- src/redactor.ts: payload preview redaction.
- src/context.ts: AsyncLocalStorage context propagation.
- src/provider.ts: provider registry.
- src/cli.ts: psy command-line interface.
- src/ingest.ts: JSONL envelope ingestion for non-Node observers.
- src/adapters/: provider-specific wrappers for Anthropic Memory, Letta, Mastra, Mem0, LangChain, LangGraph, GBrain, and related types.

## Integration docs

- python/psy-core-hermes/README.md: Hermes Agent plugin, trust-layer command, hook model, captured surfaces, operator reference.
- plugins/psy-core-openclaw/README.md: OpenClaw plugin, hook model, captured surfaces, operator reference.
- examples/hermes-agent/README.md: local Hermes walkthrough.

## Test map

- tests/*: root TypeScript and adapter tests.
- tests/e2e/ts-adapters-shared-chain.e2e.ts: shared-chain adapter e2e.
- python/psy-core-hermes/tests/: Hermes plugin tests.
- plugins/psy-core-openclaw/test/: OpenClaw plugin tests.

## Key boundaries

- psy-core is tamper-evident, not tamper-proof.
- psy-core records durable memory and skill surfaces, not every agent action.
- The wrapper path fails closed before provider execution if the intent row cannot be written.
- If the provider succeeds but the result row cannot be written, verification can flag the orphaned intent.
- Payload preview redaction is best-effort diagnostics, not a DLP guarantee.
- A stock GBrain CLI/server process is not audited unless the host imports and applies `psy-core/gbrain`.

## Voice

When summarizing psy-core, lead with the receipt: what was attempted, what happened, who/tenant/session did it, and whether the chain still verifies. Avoid broad compliance language unless the exact threat model is included.
